implementation_amendments.reason: PyTorch rejected an exact quantile on a very large CUDA weight tensor during the first pruning diagnostic.
implementation_amendments.change: Use a deterministic fixed-stride sample of at most one million magnitudes to estimate each per-tensor pruning threshold.
implementation_amendments.inferential_effect: No change to models, tasks, controls, seeds, pruning rates, budgets, thresholds, or decision rules. The incomplete first job directory was deleted and the full job restarted.

implementation_amendments.reason: The shell from the failed first attempt survived the process-level stop and began a duplicate Phi job while the clean retry was running Qwen.
implementation_amendments.change: Stop the stale shell and its child process, delete the incomplete overlapping Phi directory and log, and continue only the clean retry shell.
implementation_amendments.inferential_effect: No duplicate job completed and no overlapping Phi output was retained. The clean Qwen retry was not modified. No scientific design, budget, threshold, model, task, or decision rule changed.

implementation_amendments.reason: The deterministic pruning sampler referenced the Python math module without importing it.
implementation_amendments.change: Import the standard-library math module. The runner's automatic retry had begun with the previously loaded script, so it was stopped and the entire campaign was restarted from an empty output root after the import was verified.
implementation_amendments.inferential_effect: No Qwen attempt produced a completed job. The retained campaign began only after the import passed compilation. No scientific design, implementation behavior, budget, threshold, model, task, or decision rule changed.
